channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: India remains in contact with family of ex-naval officer detained in Qatar
item.description: The article reports that India is in touch with the family of a former Indian naval officer who has been detained in Qatar. The case is part of a wider and sensitive diplomatic matter involving former...

item.title: Islamabad Court Grants Bail to 11 Suspects in Khamenei Protest Case
item.description: On Thursday, the Anti-Terrorism Court (ATC) in Islamabad granted post-arrest bail to 11 individuals who were arrested during protests following the death of Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei. The co...

item.title: Dimsum workers win wage increase and service charge share after strike
item.description: After six days of striking, workers from Dimsum restaurants have successfully negotiated for higher wages and a share of the service charge. The labor action, which took place in several branches, foc...

item.title: Space dominance critical for India's future defence strategy, says CDS and DRDO chief
item.description: India's defence leaders have underscored the strategic importance of space in modern warfare, with Chief of Defence Staff (CDS) General Anil Chauhan highlighting that without dominance in space, India...

item.title: Australia increases fuel reserves amid Middle East conflict
item.description: Australia has significantly increased its fuel reserves, now holding 46 days' worth of petrol, which is 10 days more than prior to the outbreak of conflict in the Middle East. The war, which led to di...

item.title: US rejects nuclear option as naval tensions with Iran escalate in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The United States has intensified its military posture in the Strait of Hormuz as tensions with Iran continue, with President Donald Trump clearly ruling out the use of nuclear weapons while still sig...

item.title: New Research Reveals the Age of the Twelve Apostles and Their Formation Process
item.description: The Twelve Apostles, iconic limestone stacks along Victoria’s Great Ocean Road, have long fascinated visitors and scientists alike. A recent study published in the Australian Journal of Earth Sciences...

item.title: Nyashinski’s Wife Zia Shares Her Journey from Gikomba Hawker to Fashion Entrepreneur
item.description: Nyashinski's wife, Zia Bett, has shared the inspiring story of how she began her fashion journey from very humble beginnings. Back when she was in university, about 13 years ago, she started a small s...

item.title: 1,161 Filipino seafarers safely leave Persian Gulf amid Middle East tensions
item.description: A total of 1,161 Filipino seafarers have been safely evacuated from the Persian Gulf region amid escalating tensions in the Middle East, according to a statement from the Palace. This action was taken...

item.title: Katlego Mphela predicts Chiefs can challenge Pirates in Soweto derby
item.description: In episode 101 of the Arena Sports Show, host Clauiee Grace Mpanza and regular contributor Sizwe Mabena were joined by former Bafana Bafana striker Katlego 'Killer' Mphela. The discussion primarily fo...

item.title: India monitoring Nepal’s enforcement of customs duty rules on cross-border goods
item.description: The Ministry of External Affairs (MEA) has stated that India is closely engaging with Nepal following reports that Kathmandu may begin enforcing customs duties on goods carried by individuals travelli...

item.title: Former Foreign Office Chief Gives Testimony Highlighting Pressure from No 10
item.description: The former head of the Foreign Office, Sir Olly Robbins, appeared before a parliamentary committee to discuss the handling of Lord Peter Mandelson's security vetting. Robbins highlighted a dismissive ...

item.title: New Photos Show Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini at Sedona Resort
item.description: The New York Post has released additional photographs showing Patriots coach Mike Vrabel and former Athletic reporter Dianna Russini together at an adults-only resort in Sedona, Arizona. The new image...

item.title: Life-Size LEGO Animals Combine with Real Creatures in Creative Campaign for Twycross Zoo
item.description: A new marketing campaign for Twycross Zoo in England has caught the attention of many with its unique concept blending real animals and LEGO. Created by the Brazilian studio Platinum FMD and Daughter ...

item.title: NIA tells Delhi HC that Yasin Malik coordinated with Pakistani leadership in Jammu & Kashmir secession efforts
item.description: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) on Wednesday informed the Delhi High Court that separatist leader Yasin Malik had been in regular contact with top Pakistani leadership, including the Prime Min...

item.title: Nine law enforcement officers arrested for alleged extortion and unlawful search in Johannesburg
item.description: Nine law enforcement officers have been arrested in connection with an alleged extortion scheme at a warehouse in Crown Mines, Johannesburg. The suspects are expected to appear in the Johannesburg Mag...

item.title: NaVIC Navigation System Faces Operational Challenges with Only Three Satellites Active
item.description: India’s regional navigation system NaVIC, developed at a cost exceeding Rs 3,200 crore, is currently operational with only three functional satellites. NaVIC was designed to function optimally with a ...

item.title: Italian Fans Reject Idea of Replacing Iran in World Cup
item.description: A proposal made by Donald Trump's special envoy, Paolo Zampolli, to replace Iran with Italy at the 2026 World Cup has drawn strong reactions from Italian football fans. While Zampolli, an Italian-Amer...

item.title: More Than 50 Women Rescued from Human Trafficking in Pasig KTV Club Raid
item.description: In a recent operation by the National Bureau of Investigation (NBI), more than 50 women were rescued from a KTV club in Pasig City that was allegedly involved in sex trafficking activities. Authoritie...

item.title: Only One Applicant Approved Under Trump’s $1 Million Gold Card Visa Scheme
item.description: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick don confirm say na only one person don successfully get approval under President Donald Trump ‘gold card’ visa programme since dem launch am. This special residenc...
